summary: The system creates a governed digital twin of LK20 where national curriculum data remains canonical and local school artefacts are attached as auditable overlays.

benefits.title: Curriculum transparency
benefits.details: Grades, subjects, programmes, competence aims, assessment rules, and local plans become inspectable through one governed structure.

benefits.title: Canonical/local separation
benefits.details: Udir/Grep content remains authoritative; school uploads bind to canonical targets without mutating them.

benefits.title: Traceable local implementation
benefits.details: Annual plans, term plans, unit plans, rubrics, and evidence are hashed, logged, and linked to curriculum targets.

benefits.title: Coverage and gap detection
benefits.details: The system can identify missing grade/subject bindings and later full competence-aim coverage once Grep data is populated.

benefits.title: Teacher planning support
benefits.details: Teachers get a structured workflow for validating and attaching local plans to LK20.

benefits.title: Student-facing clarity
benefits.details: Students can view relevant learning goals, approved plans, and assessment expectations without seeing private governance data.

benefits.title: Auditability
benefits.details: Every mutating action can be written to local audit logs and every network state can be Merkle-verified.

benefits.title: Privacy boundary visibility
benefits.details: Student evidence is explicitly separated through role gates, visibility flags, and DPIA warnings.

benefits.title: Authority-level insight
benefits.details: Municipalities, counties, and national inspectors can inspect aggregated implementation status without direct access to student-level artefacts.

architecture.digital_twin_kernel.py: Governed tree tensor network and state kernel.

privacy_position: Local uploads are not public by default.
Student evidence must be marked contains_student_data=True.
Government reports should aggregate or exclude student-level artefacts.
